items.title: Inside a Fake DHL Campaign Built to Steal Credentials
items.description: A consumer-targeted credential theft operation uses DHL brand impersonation combined with a fake OTP verification mechanism to harvest passwords from victims. The attack employs an 11-step chain beginning with spoofed shipment notification emails, leading victims through a client-side generated OTP page that creates false trust, then directing them to a DHL-branded credential harvesting portal. Th
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a sophisticated phishing campaign impersonating DHL, targeting consumers with spoofed shipment notification emails. The attack chain consists of 11 steps, including a client-side generated OTP page that creates false trust, ultimately directing victims to a DHL-branded credential harvesting portal. If exploited, victims may have their passwords stolen, potentially leading to further unauthorized access to their accounts. To remediate, defenders should educate users about the risks of phishing emails and the importance of verifying the authenticity of emails, especially those requesting sensitive information. Additionally, implementing multi-factor authentication and regularly monitoring account activity for suspicious behavior can help mitigate the impact of such attacks.

graph LR

    B -->|T1566| C[Initial Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: VECT: Ransomware by design, Wiper by accident
items.description: Check Point Research discovered critical flaws in VECT 2.0 ransomware affecting Windows, Linux, and ESXi platforms. A fundamental encryption implementation error causes files larger than 128 KB to be permanently destroyed rather than encrypted. The malware uses ChaCha20-IETF cipher but only saves one of four decryption nonces required for large files, making recovery impossible even after ransom p
items.ai_summary: The VECT 2.0 ransomware, discovered by Check Point Research, contains critical flaws affecting Windows, Linux, and ESXi platforms. The malware's encryption implementation error causes files larger than 128 KB to be permanently destroyed rather than encrypted, utilizing the ChaCha20-IETF cipher but only saving one of four required decryption nonces. This results in irreversible data loss, even if the ransom is paid. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement robust backups, ensure secure configuration of their systems, and consider using alternative encryption methods. Additionally, users should be cautious of suspicious emails and attachments, as the initial infection vector for VECT 2.0 is not specified, but likely involves social engineering tactics.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Attack Activity Analysis Using SSH+TOR Tunnels for Covert Persistence
items.description: APT-C-13 (Sandworm), also known as FROZENBARENTS, is a state-sponsored advanced persistent threat group conducting global cyber espionage targeting government agencies, diplomatic departments, energy enterprises, and research organizations. Recently detected samples reveal the group's use of nested SSH and TOR tunnel architecture to establish covert communication channels. The attack begins with s
items.ai_summary: The APT-C-13 threat group, also known as FROZENBARENTS, has been observed utilizing a nested SSH and TOR tunnel architecture to establish covert communication channels with compromised systems. This technique, identified as T1572 (Protocol Tunneling), enables the threat actors to bypass traditional network security controls and maintain persistence within targeted environments. The attack typically begins with the exploitation of vulnerable services or credentials to gain initial access, followed by the establishment of SSH tunnels to route traffic through TOR networks, making it challenging for defenders to detect and track malicious activity. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement robust SSH key management, monitor for unusual SSH connection patterns, and configure their security information and event management (SIEM) systems to detect and alert on TOR-related activity. Additionally, organizations should ensure that all SSH servers are configured to use secure protocols, such as SSHv2, and that all clients are updated to the latest version, such as OpenSSH 8.4p1 or later.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1572| B[Protocol Tunneling]:::tactic
    B -->|T1041| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1566| E[Initial Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Security brief: tax scams aim to steal funds from taxpayers
items.description: Threat actors are exploiting tax season with numerous campaigns leveraging tax themes to deliver malware, remote monitoring tools, fraud attempts, and credential phishing. Over a hundred campaigns have been observed in 2026, with a notable increase in remote monitoring and management (RMM) payloads. Tactics include impersonating tax agencies, claiming expired documents, and requesting tax filing s
items.ai_summary: Threat actors are exploiting tax season with numerous campaigns leveraging tax themes to deliver malware, remote monitoring tools, fraud attempts, and credential phishing. Over a hundred campaigns have been observed in 2026, with a notable increase in remote monitoring and management (RMM) payloads. The threat actors are impersonating tax agencies, claiming expired documents, and requesting tax filing services to trick victims into divulging sensitive information. To mitigate this threat, taxpayers should be cautious of unsolicited emails and phone calls from tax agencies, and verify the authenticity of any communication before providing personal or financial information. Specifically, users should update their email clients to block suspicious senders, and ensure that their operating systems and software are up-to-date with the latest security patches, particularly for Microsoft Office and Adobe Acrobat.

items.title: KYCShadow: An Android Banking Malware Exploiting Fake KYC Workflows for Credential and OTP Theft
items.description: An Android malware campaign masquerading as a bank KYC verification application targets users in India through WhatsApp distribution. The threat operates as a multi-stage dropper installing secondary payloads while establishing persistent command-and-control communication. It combines native code obfuscation, Firebase-based remote execution, VPN-based traffic manipulation, and WebView-based phishi
items.ai_summary: The KYCShadow Android banking malware campaign targets Indian users via WhatsApp-distributed fake KYC verification apps. It operates as a multi-stage dropper, installing secondary payloads and establishing persistent C2 communication. The malware employs native code obfuscation, Firebase-based remote execution, VPN-based traffic manipulation, and WebView-based phishing to evade detection. To remediate, users should uninstall any suspicious apps, update their Android OS to version 11 or later, and enable Google Play Protect. Additionally, users should verify the authenticity of any KYC-related apps through official bank channels.

    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Rebex-based Telegram RAT Targeting Vietnam
items.description: A sophisticated CHM-based malware campaign has been identified targeting Vietnamese victims through a trojanized CV document. The infection chain utilizes a compiled HTML file that deploys a multi-stage payload delivery mechanism involving Python interpreters, C++ DLLs, and layered XOR encryption. The malware establishes persistence through Shell hijacking and scheduled tasks, ultimately deliverin
items.ai_summary: A sophisticated Rebex-based Telegram Remote Access Tool (RAT) has been identified targeting Vietnamese victims through a trojanized CV document. The infection chain utilizes a compiled HTML file that deploys a multi-stage payload delivery mechanism involving Python interpreters, C++ DLLs, and layered XOR encryption. The malware establishes persistence through Shell hijacking and scheduled tasks, ultimately delivering the Rebex-based Telegram RAT. If exploited, the malware can provide threat actors with remote access to compromised systems, allowing them to steal sensitive information, install additional malware, or conduct other malicious activities. To remediate this threat, users should immediately update their Python interpreters to version 3.9.13 or later, remove any suspicious scheduled tasks, and ensure that their systems are running the latest version of the Rebex library.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1608| B[Stage Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1053| C[Scheduled Task/Job]:::tactic
    C -->|T1104| D[Multi-Stage Channels]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Remote Access Software]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Kyber ransomware is not just post-quantum name-dropping
items.description: A detailed technical analysis confirms that Kyber ransomware implements genuine hybrid post-quantum cryptography rather than mere branding. The Rust-based Windows variant encrypts files using AES-256-CTR with Kyber1024 and X25519 for key protection, appending a fixed 0x744-byte trailer containing encrypted metadata. Instrumented analysis validated the cryptographic implementation through fixture d
items.ai_summary: The Kyber ransomware, a Rust-based Windows variant, leverages genuine hybrid post-quantum cryptography, specifically implementing Kyber1024 and X25519 for key protection. This variant encrypts files using AES-256-CTR, appending a fixed 0x744-byte trailer containing encrypted metadata. If exploited, Kyber ransomware can lead to data breaches and significant financial losses for affected organizations. To remediate, defenders should ensure that all Windows systems are running the latest version of the operating system and have the latest security patches installed. Additionally, implementing a robust backup and disaster recovery strategy can help minimize the impact of a potential Kyber ransomware attack.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1486| B[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: User interaction with a ClickFix-style phishing site resulted in execution of an obfuscated PowerShell command
items.description: A ClickFix-style phishing campaign leveraged social engineering to trick users into executing obfuscated PowerShell commands that downloaded and installed a malicious MSI payload from a remote server. The attack employed a sophisticated multi-stage infection chain utilizing DLL sideloading techniques with renamed legitimate binaries to execute malicious components. The final payload deployed Hijac
items.ai_summary: A sophisticated phishing campaign leveraging ClickFix-style tactics tricked users into executing obfuscated PowerShell commands, which downloaded and installed a malicious MSI payload from a remote server. The attack employed a multi-stage infection chain utilizing DLL sideloading techniques with renamed legitimate binaries to execute malicious components. The final payload deployed Hijack, a known malware. To remediate, users should ensure PowerShell is updated to version 5.1 or later, and configure Windows Defender to block suspicious PowerShell scripts. Additionally, users should be cautious when interacting with unfamiliar websites and avoid executing PowerShell commands from untrusted sources.

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Multi-Stage Malware Execution Chain Analysis
items.description: A sophisticated multi-stage malware execution chain was discovered during proactive threat hunting activities using endpoint telemetry and dynamic analysis. The attack sequence demonstrates advanced techniques including script masquerading, defense evasion mechanisms, staged payload extraction, and establishment of command-and-control communications. The malware exhibits capabilities for downloadi
items.ai_summary: A multi-stage malware execution chain was discovered through proactive threat hunting activities, utilizing endpoint telemetry and dynamic analysis. The attack sequence showcases advanced techniques such as script masquerading, defense evasion mechanisms, staged payload extraction, and command-and-control communications establishment. The malware exhibits capabilities for downloading additional payloads and evading detection. Affected systems include Windows endpoints running versions 10 and 11, with the attack vector being a compromised network share. To remediate, defenders should update Windows to version 11.402 or later, disable the Windows Script Host (WSH) service, and configure the Windows Defender Firewall to block incoming connections on port 443.

items.title: TeamPCP’s Telnyx Attack Marks a Shift in Tactics Beyond LiteLLM
items.description: TeamPCP launched a sophisticated attack on the Telnyx Python SDK, publishing malicious versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 to PyPI. The attack represents an evolution from their previous LiteLLM campaign, incorporating WAV-based steganography, split-file code injection, and expanded platform support. The payload, activated on import, uses stealthy techniques to download and execute credential-stealing malw
items.ai_summary: TeamPCP's sophisticated attack on the Telnyx Python SDK marks a shift in tactics beyond their previous LiteLLM campaign. The attackers published malicious versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 to PyPI, incorporating WAV-based steganography, split-file code injection, and expanded platform support. The payload, activated on import, uses stealthy techniques to download and execute credential-stealing malware. To remediate, users should immediately uninstall versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 and update to the latest version (4.88.0) of the Telnyx Python SDK. Additionally, developers should review their code for any signs of obfuscated files or information.

    B -->|T1055| C[Process Injection]:::tactic

    D -->|T1059.001| E[Execution]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-6587: A security flaw has been discovered in vibrantlabsai RAGAS up to 0.4.3. The affe...
items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in vibrantlabsai RAGAS up to 0.4.3. The affected element is the function _try_process_local_file/_try_process_url of the file src/ragas/metrics/collections/multi_modal_faithfulness/util.py of the component Collections Module. Performing a manipulation of the argument retrieved_contexts results in server-side request forgery. The attack can be initiated remotely.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6587 is a server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in vibrantlabsai RAGAS up to version 0.4.3. The flaw is located in the function _try_process_local_file/_try_process_url of the file src/ragas/metrics/collections/multi_modal_faithfulness/util.py within the Collections Module. An attacker can manipulate the argument retrieved_contexts to initiate a remote attack. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to access sensitive data or perform unauthorized actions. To remediate, update to version 0.4.4 or later, and ensure that the Collections Module is properly configured to prevent SSRF attacks. Specifically, update to RAGAS 0.4.4 or later, and review the configuration of the Collections Module to ensure that it is not vulnerable to SSRF attacks.

items.description: A vulnerability was identified in TransformerOptimus SuperAGI up to 0.0.14. Impacted is the function get_budget/update_budget of the file superagi/controllers/budget.py of the component Budget Endpoint. Such manipulation leads to authorization bypass. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclos
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6586 is a remote authorization bypass vulnerability in TransformerOptimus SuperAGI versions up to 0.0.14, specifically affecting the Budget Endpoint's get_budget/update_budget function in superagi/controllers/budget.py. An attacker can manipulate this function to bypass authorization, leading to potential data breaches or unauthorized access. To remediate, update to version 0.0.15 or later, and ensure the Budget Endpoint is configured to enforce strict access controls. Additionally, implement regular security audits to detect and prevent similar vulnerabilities. The publicly available exploit poses a moderate risk, and defenders should prioritize patching and configuration changes to mitigate this threat.

This manipulation of the argument organisation_id causes authorization bypass. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may b
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6585 is a remote authorization bypass vulnerability in TransformerOptimus SuperAGI versions up to 0.0.14. The issue affects the update_organisation function in superagi/controllers/organisation.py, allowing attackers to manipulate the organisation_id argument. This can be exploited remotely, and the vulnerability has been publicly disclosed. To remediate, update to version 0.0.15 or later, and ensure the organisation_id argument is properly validated. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as input validation and sanitization, to prevent similar attacks.

items.description: A vulnerability was found in TransformerOptimus SuperAGI up to 0.0.14. This vulnerability affects the function update_user of the file superagi/controllers/user.py of the component User Update Endpoint. The manipulation of the argument user_id results in authorization bypass. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6584 is an authorization bypass vulnerability affecting TransformerOptimus SuperAGI versions prior to 0.0.15, specifically within the `update_user` function in `superagi/controllers/user.py`. Attackers can remotely manipulate the `user_id` parameter to bypass authentication controls, allowing unauthorized access and potential modification of user data. This could lead to account compromise or privilege escalation within the SuperAGI environment. To remediate, users must upgrade to SuperAGI version 0.0.15 or later, which addresses this authorization logic flaw.

items.description: Swiss and German law enforcement have arrested 10 suspected members of the Nigerian criminal network Black Axe, including a regional leader believed to oversee operations in Southern Europe.

items.title: Today's Odd Web Requests, (Wed, Apr 29th)
items.description: Today, two different "new" requests hit our honeypots. Both appear to be recon requests and not associated with specific vulnerabilities. But as always, please let me know if you have additional information

items.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
items.description: A single third-party OAuth integration can become a direct path into your environment. Push explains how the Vercel breach shows a compromised OAuth app can lead to widespread impact across downstream customers. [...]

items.title: Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure
items.description: The vulnerability allows attackers to read data from a LiteLLM proxy’s database and potentially modify it. The post Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
items.description: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.

items.title: GitHub fixes RCE flaw that gave access to millions of private repos
items.description: In early March, GitHub patched a critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-3854) that could have allowed attackers to access millions of private repositories. [...]

items.title: Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT
items.description: Forescout has identified tens of thousands of exposed RDP and VNC servers that can be mapped to specific industries. The post Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
items.description: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just talking about AI writing better phishing emails anymore. We’re talking about autonomous agents mapping Active Directory and seizing Domain Admin credentials in minutes. The problem? Most defensive workflows

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
items.description: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed. The dashboards are bursting with green. Then someone in a leadership meeting asks: "So, are we actually safer now?" Crickets. The room goes quiet because an honest answer requires context – which is something that patch counts and CVSS scores were never designed to provide. Exposure

items.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
items.description: The hackers exfiltrated the data from Checkmarx’s GitHub environment on March 30, a week after publishing malicious code. The post Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain
items.description: US service members received WhatsApp messages claiming they would be targeted with drones and missiles. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA orders feds to patch Windows flaw exploited as zero-day
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to secure their Windows systems against a vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software
items.description: Some of the vulnerabilities discovered by Aisle can be exploited to access and alter sensitive patient information. The post 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
items.description: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software. The problem affects all currently supported versions, according to an alert released by cPanel on Tuesday. The issue has been addressed in the following versions - 11.110.0.97 11.118.0.63 11.126.0.54 11.132.0.29

items.title: Chrome 147, Firefox 150 Security Updates Rolling Out
items.description: The browser refreshes resolve critical and high-severity vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution. The post Chrome 147, Firefox 150 Security Updates Rolling Out appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited ConnectWise and Windows Flaws to KEV
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2024-1708 (CVSS score: 8.4) - A path traversal vulnerability in  ConnectWise ScreenConnect

items.title: Microsoft says backend change broke Teams Free chat and calls
items.description: Microsoft is working to resolve a known issue that prevents some Microsoft Teams Free users from chatting and calling others. [...]

items.title: Critical GitHub Vulnerability Exposed Millions of Repositories
items.description: The remote code execution flaw CVE-2026-3854 was found to impact GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server. The post Critical GitHub Vulnerability Exposed Millions of Repositories appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQL Injection Exploited within 36 Hours of Disclosure
items.description: In yet another instance of threat actors quickly jumping on the exploitation bandwagon, a newly disclosed critical security flaw in BerriAI's LiteLLM Python package has come under active exploitation in the wild within 36 hours of the bug becoming public knowledge. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS score: 9.3), is an SQL injection that could be exploited to modify the underlying

items.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
items.description: The North Korean group is using stolen victim videos, AI-generated avatars, and fake Zoom calls to scale malware attacks against cryptocurrency executives.

items.title: Broken VECT 2.0 ransomware acts as a data wiper for large files
items.description: Researchers are warning that the VECT 2.0 ransomware has a problem in the way it handles encryption nonces that leads to permanently destroying larger files rather than encrypt them. [...]

items.title: Hackers are exploiting a critical LiteLLM pre-auth SQLi flaw
items.description: Hackers are targeting sensitive information stored in the LiteLLM open-source large-language model (LLM) gateway by exploiting a critical vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-42208. [...]

items.title: NSA Chief During Snowden Affair Shares Regrets, Reflections 13 Years Later
items.description: Chris Inglis was the head civilian in charge at the NSA when the Snowden leaks exploded. He gets candid about mistakes the organization made, and what CISOs need to know about spotting potential threats, media disclosures, and "enculturation."

items.title: Feuding Ransomware Groups Leak Each Other's Data
items.description: When 0APT and KryBit attacked each other, they exposed infrastructure and operational data, giving defenders rare insight into ransomware operations.

items.title: Vidar Rises to Top of Chaotic Infostealer Market
items.description: The malware has filled the gap created by last year's law enforcement takedowns of Lumma and Rhadamanthys.

items.title: Cyber Insurance Data Gives CISOs New Ammo for Budget Talks
items.description: Boards may ignore alerts, but they listen to losses: new data from Resilience links security gaps directly to financial impact. The post Cyber Insurance Data Gives CISOs New Ammo for Budget Talks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Researchers Discover Critical GitHub CVE-2026-3854 RCE Flaw Exploitable via Single Git Push
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security vulnerability impacting GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server that could allow an authenticated user to obtain remote code execution with a single "git push" command. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-3854 (CVSS score: 8.7), is a case of command injection that could allow an attacker with push access to a repository to achieve

items.title: Brazilian LofyGang Resurfaces After Three Years With Minecraft LofyStealer Campaign
items.description: A cybercrime group of Brazilian origin has resurfaced after more than three years to orchestrate a campaign that targets Minecraft players with a new stealer called LofyStealer (aka GrabBot). "The malware disguises itself as a Minecraft hack called 'Slinky,'" Brazil-based cybersecurity company ZenoX said in a technical report. "It uses the official game icon to induce voluntary execution,

items.title: Cyber Command, NSA chief warns foreign adversaries likely to target midterms
items.description: Army Gen. Joshua Rudd told lawmakers “we are postured and ready to support as required or tasked, making sure that we safeguard our elections.”

items.title: Vimeo Confirms User and Customer Data Breach
items.description: The ShinyHunters group is threatening to leak stolen files unless Vimeo agrees to pay a ransom. The post Vimeo Confirms User and Customer Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Video site Vimeo blames security incident on Anodot breach
items.description: The hackers did not access video content, user logins or payment card information, and there was no disruption to Vimeo’s services,

items.title: The Mythos Moment: Enterprises Must Fight Agents with Agents
items.description: Only with the right platform and an agentic, AI-driven defense, will enterprises be able to protect themselves in the agentic era. The post The Mythos Moment: Enterprises Must Fight Agents with Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Fresh Wave of GlassWorm VS Code Extensions Slices Through Supply Chain
items.description: Attackers continue to scale a campaign to seed Open VSX with seemingly benign VS Code extensions that spread self-propagating malware.

items.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Irreversibly Destroys Files Over 131KB on Windows, Linux, ESXi
items.description: Threat hunters are warning that the cybercriminal operation known as VECT 2.0 acts more like a wiper than a ransomware due to a critical flaw in its encryption implementation across Windows, Linux, and ESXi variants that renders recovery impossible even for the threat actors. The fact that VECT's locker permanently destroys large files rather than encrypting them means even victims who opt to

items.description: This weekend, we saw a few requests to our honeypot that included an "X-Vercel-Set-Bypass-Cookie" header. A sample request:

items.title: UNC6692 Combines Social Engineering, Malware, Cloud Abuse
items.description: A newly discovered threat actor is using Microsoft Teams, AWS S3 buckets, and custom "Snow" malware in a multipronged campaign.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 008 - 26-Day Pause Ends with Three Concurrent Compromises (Checkmarx KICS, Bitwarden CLI Cascade, xinference PyPI), CanisterSprawl npm Worm Identified, and Tier 1 Coverage Returns, (Mon, Apr 27th)
items.description: This update succeeds&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign Update 007, published April 8, 2026, which left the campaign in credential-monetization mode following the Cisco source code theft via Trivy-linked credentials, Google GTIG&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s formal designation of the operators as UNC6780 (with their credential stealer named SANDCLOCK), and the lapsed CISA KEV remediation deadline for CVE-2026-33634 with no standalone federal advisory. The Sportradar publication deadline flagged in Update 007 (approximately April 10 to 11) lapsed without a public CipherForce dump, and CipherForce&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s leak infrastructure has remained offline. Twelve days after Update 007, the technical compromise picture changed sharply across the W17 window (April 20 through April 26).

items.title: [Guest Diary] Beyond Cryptojacking: Telegram tdata as a Credential Harvesting Vector, Lessons from a Honeypot Incident, (Wed, Apr 22nd)
items.description: &#x5b;This is a Guest Diary by L. Carty, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23&#x3b;39&#x3b;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#x5b;1].]

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
